# __MAR_APP_NAME__ — iOS

This Xcode project was generated by `mar build --target ios`. **Do not
edit the Swift sources by hand** — they're rewritten on every build.
The whole point is that customizing your app means changing your mar
code, not the iOS scaffolding.

## Open & build

Requires [XcodeGen](https://github.com/yonaskolb/XcodeGen) and Xcode 15+.

```sh
brew install xcodegen           # one-time
xcodegen generate               # produces __MAR_APP_NAME__.xcodeproj
open __MAR_APP_NAME__.xcodeproj
```

In Xcode, set your signing team under Signing & Capabilities, then
build & run on a simulator or device.

## How it works

The Swift app is a generic shell that, on cold start, fetches
`/_mar/schema` from the configured backend (default
`__MAR_DEFAULT_BASE_URL__`, overridable in Settings). Whatever
services and routes your mar app exposes show up as native UI without
any iOS code changes. That's the **OTA model** — change your mar code,
restart the iOS app, see new behavior. No App Store re-submission.

## Pointing at a different backend

Two ways:

1. At generation time: `mar build --target ios --base-url https://api.example.com`
2. At runtime: open the **Settings** tab in the app and edit the Base
   URL. Saved to UserDefaults; survives app restarts.
